title: Malache fruticosa
intro: Malachra fruticosa, commonly known as the rough horsewood or rough horse-wood tree, is a tropical plant species native to Australia and Asia, characterized by its small, greenish-yellow flowers and edible fruit. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal and culinary properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Malachra fruticosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is commonly used in the form of teas, tinctures, and ointments.
taxonomy: Malache fruticosa is classified as a type of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. It belongs to the genus Malachra, which includes several species of tropical plants. The exact classification of Malache fruticosa is Malachra fruticosa, with its full botanical name being Malachra fruticosa. Further classification also places it under the order Lamiales and the class Magnoliopsida. It is a member of the family Bignoniaceae, which is commonly known as the Bignonia family.
